text.title: market-sizing-with-ai Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for market-sizing-with-ai as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: AI tools confuse TAM, SAM, and SOM if not defined explicitly in the prompt.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: LLMs use these terms interchangeably in training data; without explicit definitions in the prompt, output will conflate them and produce inconsistent estimates.

failure-mode.description: Bottom-up estimates require accurate counts of target customers; AI will guess if no reliable dataset exists.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Treat customer count as a human-supplied input, not AI output. The agent cannot verify customer count.

failure-mode.description: Statista and CB Insights data lags 6-18 months; AI-synthesized estimates inherit that lag without disclosure.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Source recency must be verified by the agent and reported alongside the estimate; stale sources must be flagged explicitly.

failure-mode.description: Triangulation only improves confidence if the two paths are truly independent — AI often draws from the same underlying sources for both.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Instruct the agent explicitly to use different source sets for each path; verify source independence before trusting the convergence.

failure-mode.description: Currency and geography scope creep: AI frequently extends a US-market figure to global without flagging the assumption.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Geographic scope must be declared in the prompt as a hard constraint, not inferred, to prevent silent extrapolation.

failure-mode.description: Agents conflate TAM and SAM without explicit definitions; define both in the system prompt.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Both definitions must be injected in the system prompt on every run; they are not preserved across sessions.

failure-mode.description: LLMs anchor heavily on the first data point encountered; instruct the agent to collect all sources before synthesizing.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Anchoring bias from early data inflates confidence; collecting all sources before producing any number is not the default behavior.

failure-mode.description: Bottom-up paths require a customer count estimate the agent cannot verify; treat this as a human-supplied input, not AI output.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: The agent cannot independently verify customer counts for a target market segment.

failure-mode.description: "Similar market" reasoning (e.g., "CRM market is $X, so AI research tools should be Y% of that") is a hallucination risk unless sourced.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Require the agent to cite its source for any comparable market used in reasoning; prevent inference without attribution.

failure-mode.description: Models will omit the confidence range unless explicitly required in the output schema.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Point estimates invite false precision; confidence ranges must be a required field in the output schema, not an optional addition.
